confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_81d2e767-9f18-4acf-8e8e-bf3ae9a8845d read back as received

payload.authority_note: Operator requested submission after observing ergonomics issues during task 55 commissioning.

observations.detail: Calling narada task create --input-json from Windows with a path translated through wslpath failed because the path was malformed; the resulting CLI error was --title is required, which points at payload shape rather than unreadable input path.
observations.finding: Windows path translation failure produced a misleading task-create error
observations.impact: Architects waste attention diagnosing the wrong layer and may retry with ad hoc shell handling.

observations.detail: Several recent tasks were created with Required Work: 1. TBD. The workboard now correctly reports them as underspecified, but the authoring command still permits non-actionable handoffs by default.
observations.finding: task create defaults to placeholder Required Work
observations.impact: Builder loops can see work that is formally present but not executable, increasing operator/architect burden.

observations.detail: workboard shows narada task amend <n> --required-work <actionable-work-plan>, but there is no single guided command that converts current task intent into an actionable handoff packet safely.
observations.finding: workboard repair guidance is diagnostic, not authoring workflow
observations.impact: Architect has to hand-author repair text and remember the exact shape required by task actionability.

requests.text: Add a Windows-safe task authoring wrapper or CLI mode that accepts native Windows paths and reports unreadable input-json paths as path/input errors, not missing title errors.
Change task authoring so placeholder Required Work is refused for normal executable tasks unless the task is explicitly marked draft/sketch/non-executable.
Provide a guided repair command for underspecified tasks, e.g. task make-actionable or task amend --from-intent, that replaces placeholder Required Work and rechecks handoff actionability.

payload.summary: During User Site commissioning, Windows-side task creation exposed path translation friction and placeholder Required Work drift. Narada should provide a first-class Windows-safe authoring path and stronger prevention of non-actionable task handoffs.
payload.title: Improve Windows task authoring and actionable handoff ergonomics
